title: 1st Amendment
gist: Empowered the state to make special provisions for the advancement of socially and educationally backward classes.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Enacted to overcome judicial decisions regarding fundamental rights, it empowered the state to make special provisions for backward classes and protected land reform laws.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (1st Amendment) Act, 1951
  • • Implementation: 18 June 1951
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Article 15(4) (New)Empowered state to make special provisions for socially/educationally backward classes.
Article 19(2)Added 3 new grounds of restriction on freedom of speech: public order, friendly relations with foreign states, and incitement to an offence.
Art 31A & 31B (New)Protected land reform laws from judicial review.
9th Schedule (New)Created a vault to protect laws included in it from fundamental rights challenges.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Paved the way for Zamindari abolition.
  • Established the first quotas/reservations in educational institutions.

title: 2nd Amendment
gist: Adjusted the scale of representation in the Lok Sabha.
details:
The Constitution (2nd Amendment) Act, 1952:
- Adjusted the scale of representation in the Lok Sabha by removing the upper limit of one member for every 7.5 lakh people.

title: 3rd Amendment
gist: Empowered Parliament to control the production, supply, and distribution of essential commodities.
details:
The Constitution (3rd Amendment) Act, 1954:
- Empowered Parliament to control the production, supply, and distribution of essential commodities.

title: 4th Amendment
gist: Restricted the right to property and limited the scope of judicial review.
details:
The Constitution (4th Amendment) Act, 1955:
- Restricted the right to property and limited the scope of judicial review regarding compensation for compulsory acquisition of property by the state.

title: 5th Amendment
gist: Authorized the President to set a time limit for State Legislatures to express their views on proposed Central laws.
details:
The Constitution (5th Amendment) Act, 1955:
- Authorized the President to set a time limit for State Legislatures to express their views on proposed Central laws affecting their boundaries.

title: 6th Amendment
gist: Included a new entry in the Union List regarding taxes on the sale or purchase of goods in inter-state trade.
details:
The Constitution (6th Amendment) Act, 1956:
- Included a new entry in the Union List regarding taxes on the sale or purchase of goods in the course of inter-state trade.

title: 7th Amendment
gist: State Reorganization: Reorganized state boundaries on linguistic lines and introduced the concept of Union Territories.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Enacted to implement the recommendations of the States Reorganisation Commission (SRC), restructuring India on linguistic lines.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (7th Amendment) Act, 1956
  • • Implementation: 1 November 1956
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Parts A, B, C, D StatesAbolished the existing classification of states. Created 14 States and 6 Union Territories.
Article 153 & 231Allowed the same person to be appointed as Governor for two or more states, and a common High Court for two or more states.
Article 350A & 350BAdded provisions for instruction in mother-tongue at primary stage and a Special Officer for Linguistic Minorities.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Redrew the political map of India based on language.
  • Established the institution of Union Territories (UTs).

title: 8th Amendment
gist: Extended the reservation of seats for SCs, STs, and Anglo-Indians in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies.
details:
The Constitution (8th Amendment) Act, 1960:
- Extended the reservation of seats for SCs, STs, and Anglo-Indians in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies for another 10 years (until 1970).

title: 9th Amendment
gist: Facilitated the transfer of certain territories to Pakistan as per the Indo-Pakistan Agreement (1958).
details:
The Constitution (9th Amendment) Act, 1960:
- Facilitated the transfer of certain territories to Pakistan as per the Indo-Pakistan Agreement (1958).

title: 10th Amendment
gist: Formally incorporated Dadra and Nagar Haveli into the Union of India as a Union Territory.
details:
The Constitution (10th Amendment) Act, 1961:
- Formally incorporated Dadra and Nagar Haveli into the Union of India as a Union Territory.

title: 11th Amendment
gist: Changed the procedure for electing the Vice-President.
details:
The Constitution (11th Amendment) Act, 1961:
- Changed the procedure for electing the Vice-President and clarified that the election of the President/Vice-President cannot be challenged based on vacancies in the electoral college.

title: 12th Amendment
gist: Incorporated Goa, Daman, and Diu into the Indian Union.
details:
The Constitution (12th Amendment) Act, 1962:
- Incorporated Goa, Daman, and Diu into the Indian Union following their liberation from Portuguese rule.

title: 13th Amendment
gist: Created the State of Nagaland with special administrative provisions under Article 371A.
details:
The Constitution (13th Amendment) Act, 1962:
- Created the State of Nagaland with special administrative provisions under Article 371A.

title: 14th Amendment
gist: Incorporated Pondicherry (now Puducherry) into the Union.
details:
The Constitution (14th Amendment) Act, 1962:
- Incorporated Pondicherry (now Puducherry) into the Union and provided for legislatures and councils of ministers for certain Union Territories.

title: 15th Amendment
gist: Raised the retirement age of High Court judges from 60 to 62 years.
details:
The Constitution (15th Amendment) Act, 1963:
- Raised the retirement age of High Court judges from 60 to 62 years and empowered HCs to issue writs outside their jurisdiction if the cause of action arises within their territory.

title: 16th Amendment
gist: Strengthened national sovereignty by requiring loyalty oaths for public officials.
details:
The Constitution (16th Amendment) Act, 1963:
- Strengthened national sovereignty by requiring loyalty oaths for public officials and allowing "reasonable restrictions" on freedom of speech in the interest of India's integrity.

title: 17th Amendment
gist: Protected 44 additional land reform acts by placing them in the Ninth Schedule.
details:
The Constitution (17th Amendment) Act, 1964:
- Protected 44 additional land reform acts by placing them in the Ninth Schedule and prohibited the acquisition of land held under personal cultivation unless compensation is paid at market value.

title: 18th Amendment
gist: Clarified Parliament's power to form new states by uniting parts of existing states/UTs.
details:
The Constitution (18th Amendment) Act, 1966:
- Clarified that the power of Parliament to form new states includes the power to create a new state or UT by uniting parts of existing states/UTs.

title: 19th Amendment
gist: Abolished Election Tribunals and transferred the power to hear election petitions to the High Courts.
details:
The Constitution (19th Amendment) Act, 1966:
- Abolished Election Tribunals and transferred the power to hear election petitions to the High Courts.

title: 20th Amendment
gist: Validated the appointments and judgments of certain district judges in Uttar Pradesh.
details:
The Constitution (20th Amendment) Act, 1966:
- Validated the appointments and judgments of certain district judges in Uttar Pradesh that had been declared invalid by the Supreme Court.

title: 21st Amendment
gist: Included Sindhi as the 15th language in the Eighth Schedule.
details:
The Constitution (21st Amendment) Act, 1967:
- Included Sindhi as the 15th language in the Eighth Schedule.

title: 22nd Amendment
gist: Facilitated the creation of Meghalaya as an autonomous state within the state of Assam.
details:
The Constitution (22nd Amendment) Act, 1969:
- Facilitated the creation of Meghalaya as an autonomous state within the state of Assam.

title: 23rd Amendment
gist: Extended SC/ST and Anglo-Indian reservations in legislatures for another 10 years.
details:
The Constitution (23rd Amendment) Act, 1969:
- Extended SC/ST and Anglo-Indian reservations in legislatures for another 10 years (until 1980).

title: 24th Amendment
gist: Affirmed Parliament's power to amend any part of the Constitution, including Fundamental Rights.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Passed to overturn the Supreme Court's Golaknath judgment, it affirmed Parliament's absolute power to amend any part of the Constitution, including Fundamental Rights.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (24th Amendment) Act, 1971
  • • Implementation: 5 November 1971
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Article 13 (Amended)Added a clause stating that nothing in Article 13 shall apply to any amendment made under Article 368.
Article 368 (Amended)Explicitly gave Parliament the power to "amend by way of addition, variation or repeal" any provision. Made President's assent to amendment bills mandatory.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Reasserted Parliamentary supremacy over the Constitution.
  • Set the stage for the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973), which later introduced the Basic Structure Doctrine.

title: 25th Amendment
gist: Restricted property rights and protected laws implementing Directive Principles.
details:
The Constitution (25th Amendment) Act, 1971:
- Restricted property rights and stipulated that laws made to implement Directive Principles (Art 39b & c) cannot be challenged for violating Fundamental Rights (Art 14, 19, 31).

title: 26th Amendment
gist: Abolished Privy Purses and the special privileges granted to former rulers of princely states.
details:
The Constitution (26th Amendment) Act, 1971:
- Abolished Privy Purses and the special privileges previously granted to the former rulers of princely states.

title: 27th Amendment
gist: Reorganized North-Eastern areas; created the UTs of Mizoram and Arunachal Pradesh.
details:
The Constitution (27th Amendment) Act, 1971:
- Reorganized North-Eastern areas; created the UTs of Mizoram and Arunachal Pradesh.

title: 28th Amendment
gist: Abolished the special privileges and conditions of service for Indian Civil Service (ICS) officers.
details:
The Constitution (28th Amendment) Act, 1972:
- Abolished the special privileges and conditions of service for Indian Civil Service (ICS) officers.

title: 29th Amendment
gist: Added two Kerala land reform amendment acts to the Ninth Schedule.
details:
The Constitution (29th Amendment) Act, 1972:
- Added two Kerala land reform amendment acts to the Ninth Schedule.

title: 30th Amendment
gist: Modified the basis for civil appeals to the Supreme Court.
details:
The Constitution (30th Amendment) Act, 1972:
- Modified the basis for civil appeals to the Supreme Court, removing the monetary threshold and basing it on "substantial questions of law."

title: 31st Amendment
gist: Increased the number of Lok Sabha seats from 525 to 545.
details:
The Constitution (31st Amendment) Act, 1973:
- Increased the number of Lok Sabha seats from 525 to 545 to reflect population growth.

title: 32nd Amendment
gist: Provided special protections for the Telangana region in Andhra Pradesh.
details:
The Constitution (32nd Amendment) Act, 1974:
- Provided special protections for the Telangana region in Andhra Pradesh to satisfy regional aspirations.

title: 33rd Amendment
gist: Mandated conditions for the validity of resignation of Parliament or State Legislature members.
details:
The Constitution (33rd Amendment) Act, 1974:
- Mandated that the resignation of a member of Parliament or State Legislature is only valid if the Presiding Officer is satisfied it is voluntary and genuine.

title: 34th Amendment
gist: Included 20 more land reform and tenure acts in the Ninth Schedule.
details:
The Constitution (34th Amendment) Act, 1974:
- Included 20 more land reform and tenure acts in the Ninth Schedule.

title: 35th Amendment
gist: Terminated the protectorate status of Sikkim and designated it as an "Associate State".
details:
The Constitution (35th Amendment) Act, 1975:
- Terminated the protectorate status of Sikkim and designated it as an "Associate State" of the Indian Union.

title: 36th Amendment
gist: Sikkim became a full-fledged state of the Indian Union.
details:
The Constitution (36th Amendment) Act, 1975:
- Sikkim became a full-fledged state of the Indian Union.

title: 37th Amendment
gist: Provided a Legislative Assembly and Council of Ministers for the UT of Arunachal Pradesh.
details:
The Constitution (37th Amendment) Act, 1975:
- Provided a Legislative Assembly and Council of Ministers for the UT of Arunachal Pradesh.

title: 38th Amendment
gist: Made the declaration of Emergency by the President non-justiciable.
details:
The Constitution (38th Amendment) Act, 1975:
- Made the declaration of Emergency by the President non-justiciable (cannot be challenged in court).

title: 39th Amendment
gist: Placed the election of the President, VP, PM, and Speaker beyond the jurisdiction of the courts.
details:
The Constitution (39th Amendment) Act, 1975:
- Placed the election of the President, VP, PM, and Speaker beyond the jurisdiction of the courts. (Later struck down by SC).

title: 40th Amendment
gist: Empowered Parliament to specify the limits of the territorial waters, continental shelf, and EEZ.
details:
The Constitution (40th Amendment) Act, 1976:
- Empowered Parliament to specify the limits of the territorial waters, continental shelf, and exclusive economic zone (EEZ).

title: 41st Amendment
gist: Raised the retirement age for members of State Public Service Commissions.
details:
The Constitution (41st Amendment) Act, 1976:
- Raised the retirement age for members of State Public Service Commissions and Joint Public Service Commissions from 60 to 62.

title: 42nd Amendment
gist: "The Mini Constitution": Added "Socialist," "Secular," and "Integrity" to the Preamble.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Enacted during the Internal Emergency, this comprehensive amendment is widely known as the "Mini-Constitution". Its primary objective was to assert the supremacy of Parliament, curtail judicial review, and formally embed socialist and secular ideals.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (42nd Amendment) Act, 1976
  • • Implementation: 18 December 1976
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
PreambleAdded "Socialist", "Secular", and "Integrity".
Part IVA (Art 51A)Inserted 10 Fundamental Duties for citizens.
Article 74Made the advice of the Council of Ministers binding on the President.
Article 368Asserted that Constitutional amendments cannot be questioned in any court.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Elevated Parliament's power above the Judiciary.
  • Extended the Lok Sabha tenure from 5 to 6 years.

title: 43rd Amendment
gist: Restored the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court and High Courts regarding judicial review.
details:
The Constitution (43rd Amendment) Act, 1977:
- Restored the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court and High Courts regarding judicial review and the issuance of writs (reversing parts of the 42nd Amendment).

title: 44th Amendment
gist: Reversed several 42nd Amendment provisions and removed "Right to Property" as a Fundamental Right.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Enacted post-Emergency, its primary objective was to reverse the excesses of the 42nd Amendment and restore democratic safeguards and civil liberties.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (44th Amendment) Act, 1978
  • • Implementation: 1979
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Article 31 & 300ARemoved the Right to Property from Fundamental Rights and made it a legal right (Art 300A).
Article 352Replaced "internal disturbance" with "armed rebellion" for declaring National Emergency. Required written cabinet advice.
Article 74Empowered the President to send cabinet advice back for reconsideration once.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Restored the Lok Sabha tenure back to 5 years.
  • Restored the power of judicial review for the Supreme Court.

title: 45th Amendment
gist: Extended SC/ST and Anglo-Indian reservations in legislatures for another 10 years.
details:
The Constitution (45th Amendment) Act, 1980:
- Extended SC/ST and Anglo-Indian reservations in legislatures for another 10 years (until 1990).

title: 46th Amendment
gist: Empowered states to plug loopholes in sales tax and levy taxes on "deemed sales".
details:
The Constitution (46th Amendment) Act, 1982:
- Empowered states to plug loopholes in sales tax and levy taxes on "deemed sales" like work contracts and food in restaurants.

title: 47th Amendment
gist: Included 14 more land reform acts from various states in the Ninth Schedule.
details:
The Constitution (47th Amendment) Act, 1984:
- Included 14 more land reform acts from various states in the Ninth Schedule.

title: 48th Amendment
gist: Permitted the extension of President's Rule in Punjab beyond one year.
details:
The Constitution (48th Amendment) Act, 1984:
- Permitted the extension of President's Rule in Punjab beyond one year without meeting the standard conditions.

title: 49th Amendment
gist: Gave constitutional sanctity to the Tripura Tribal Areas Autonomous District Council.
details:
The Constitution (49th Amendment) Act, 1984:
- Gave constitutional sanctity to the Tripura Tribal Areas Autonomous District Council.

title: 50th Amendment
gist: Empowered Parliament to restrict the Fundamental Rights of persons employed in intelligence organizations.
details:
The Constitution (50th Amendment) Act, 1984:
- Empowered Parliament to restrict the Fundamental Rights of persons employed in intelligence organizations or telecommunication systems set up for the armed forces.

title: 51st Amendment
gist: Reserved seats for STs in the Lok Sabha for Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Mizoram, and Nagaland.
details:
The Constitution (51st Amendment) Act, 1984:
- Reserved seats for STs in the Lok Sabha for Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Mizoram, and Nagaland.

title: 52nd Amendment
gist: Anti-Defection Law: Added the Tenth Schedule to disqualify members on grounds of defection.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Popularly known as the Anti-Defection Law, it was introduced to prevent political defections driven by the lure of office or material benefits.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (52nd Amendment) Act, 1985
  • • Implementation: 1 March 1985
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Articles 101, 102, 190, 191Amended to include disqualification of members of Parliament and State Legislatures on grounds of defection.
10th Schedule (New)Added the Anti-Defection framework, detailing conditions for disqualification and exemptions (like splits/mergers).
4. Functional Impact:
  • Brought stability to governments by curbing the "Aaya Ram Gaya Ram" culture.
  • Made the Presiding Officer (Speaker/Chairman) the deciding authority on defections.

title: 53rd Amendment
gist: Provided special provisions for Mizoram and fixed the minimum strength of its Assembly at 40 members.
details:
The Constitution (53rd Amendment) Act, 1986:
- Provided special provisions for Mizoram and fixed the minimum strength of its Assembly at 40 members.

title: 54th Amendment
gist: Increased the salaries of Supreme Court and High Court judges.
details:
The Constitution (54th Amendment) Act, 1986:
- Increased the salaries of Supreme Court and High Court judges and allowed for future changes without constitutional amendment.

title: 55th Amendment
gist: Granted statehood to Arunachal Pradesh.
details:
The Constitution (55th Amendment) Act, 1987:
- Granted statehood to Arunachal Pradesh.

title: 56th Amendment
gist: Granted statehood to Goa and created the UT of Daman and Diu.
details:
The Constitution (56th Amendment) Act, 1987:
- Granted statehood to Goa and created the UT of Daman and Diu.

title: 57th Amendment
gist: Reserved seats for STs in the Legislative Assemblies of Arunachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Mizoram, and Nagaland.
details:
The Constitution (57th Amendment) Act, 1987:
- Reserved seats for STs in the Legislative Assemblies of Arunachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Mizoram, and Nagaland.

title: 58th Amendment
gist: Provided for an authoritative text of the Constitution in Hindi.
details:
The Constitution (58th Amendment) Act, 1987:
- Provided for an authoritative text of the Constitution in Hindi.

title: 59th Amendment
gist: Allowed for the declaration of internal emergency in Punjab and extended President's Rule there up to three years.
details:
The Constitution (59th Amendment) Act, 1988:
- Allowed for the declaration of internal emergency in Punjab and extended President's Rule there up to three years.

title: 60th Amendment
gist: Increased the ceiling on profession tax from ₹250 to ₹2,500 per annum.
details:
The Constitution (60th Amendment) Act, 1988:
- Increased the ceiling on profession tax from ₹250 to ₹2,500 per annum.

title: 61st Amendment
gist: Reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years for Lok Sabha and State Assembly elections.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Lowered the voting age in India, expanding the democratic base by empowering the youth to participate in elections.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (61st Amendment) Act, 1988
  • • Implementation: 28 March 1989
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Article 326 (Amended)Amended to reduce the voting age from 21 years to 18 years for elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Granted voting rights to millions of young Indians.
  • Led to the Election Commission becoming a multi-member body due to the increased workload.

title: 62nd Amendment
gist: Extended SC/ST and Anglo-Indian reservations in legislatures for another 10 years (until 2000).
details:
The Constitution (62nd Amendment) Act, 1989:
- Extended SC/ST and Anglo-Indian reservations in legislatures for another 10 years (until 2000).

title: 63rd Amendment
gist: Repealed the 59th Amendment, restoring the status of Emergency provisions in Punjab.
details:
The Constitution (63rd Amendment) Act, 1989:
- Repealed the 59th Amendment, restoring the status of Emergency provisions in Punjab to be the same as the rest of the country.

title: 64th Amendment
gist: Extended President's Rule in Punjab for a further period of six months.
details:
The Constitution (64th Amendment) Act, 1990:
- Extended President's Rule in Punjab for a further period of six months.

title: 65th Amendment
gist: Established a high-level multi-member National Commission for SCs and STs.
details:
The Constitution (65th Amendment) Act, 1990:
- Established a high-level multi-member National Commission for SCs and STs, replacing the single Special Officer.

title: 66th Amendment
gist: Included 55 more land reform acts in the Ninth Schedule.
details:
The Constitution (66th Amendment) Act, 1990:
- Included 55 more land reform acts in the Ninth Schedule.

title: 67th Amendment
gist: Extended President's Rule in Punjab for another six months.
details:
The Constitution (67th Amendment) Act, 1990:
- Extended President's Rule in Punjab for another six months.

title: 68th Amendment
gist: Further extended President's Rule in Punjab for a total period of five years.
details:
The Constitution (68th Amendment) Act, 1991:
- Further extended President's Rule in Punjab for a total period of five years.

title: 69th Amendment
gist: Granted special status to Delhi, providing it with a Legislative Assembly and Council of Ministers.
details:
The Constitution (69th Amendment) Act, 1991:
- National Capital Territory (NCT): Granted special status to Delhi, providing it with a Legislative Assembly and a Council of Ministers.

title: 70th Amendment
gist: Included members of the Legislative Assemblies of Delhi and Pondicherry in the electoral college for the President.
details:
The Constitution (70th Amendment) Act, 1992:
- Included members of the Legislative Assemblies of Delhi and Pondicherry in the electoral college for the election of the President.

title: 71st Amendment
gist: Included Konkani, Manipuri, and Nepali in the Eighth Schedule.
details:
The Constitution (71st Amendment) Act, 1992:
- Included Konkani, Manipuri, and Nepali in the Eighth Schedule.

title: 72nd Amendment
gist: Provided for the reservation of seats for STs in the Legislative Assembly of Tripura.
details:
The Constitution (72nd Amendment) Act, 1992:
- Provided for the reservation of seats for STs in the Legislative Assembly of Tripura.

title: 73rd Amendment
gist: Granted constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions.
details:
The Constitution (73rd Amendment) Act, 1993:
- Panchayati Raj: Granted constitutional status to Panchayats and added Part IX and the Eleventh Schedule.

title: 74th Amendment
gist: Granted constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies (Municipalities).
details:
The Constitution (74th Amendment) Act, 1993:
- Municipalities: Granted constitutional status to urban local bodies and added Part IXA and the Twelfth Schedule.

title: 75th Amendment
gist: Provided for the setting up of State-level Rent Tribunals for adjudication of rent control disputes.
details:
The Constitution (75th Amendment) Act, 1994:
- Provided for the setting up of State-level Rent Tribunals for adjudication of rent control disputes.

title: 76th Amendment
gist: Placed the Tamil Nadu Reservation Act (69% reservation) in the Ninth Schedule.
details:
The Constitution (76th Amendment) Act, 1994:
- Placed the Tamil Nadu Reservation Act (69% reservation) in the Ninth Schedule to protect it from judicial review.

title: 77th Amendment
gist: Provided for reservation in promotions for SCs and STs in government services.
details:
The Constitution (77th Amendment) Act, 1995:
- Provided for reservation in promotions for SCs and STs in government services.

title: 78th Amendment
gist: Included 27 more land reform acts in the Ninth Schedule.
details:
The Constitution (78th Amendment) Act, 1995:
- Included 27 more land reform acts in the Ninth Schedule.

title: 79th Amendment
gist: Extended SC/ST and Anglo-Indian reservations in legislatures for another 10 years (until 2010).
details:
The Constitution (79th Amendment) Act, 1999:
- Extended SC/ST and Anglo-Indian reservations in legislatures for another 10 years (until 2010).

title: 80th Amendment
gist: Implemented an alternative scheme of sharing taxes between the Union and the States.
details:
The Constitution (80th Amendment) Act, 2000:
- Implemented an alternative scheme of sharing taxes between the Union and the States based on the 10th Finance Commission recommendations.

title: 81st Amendment
gist: Allowed the state to treat "backlog vacancies" for SC/STs as a separate class.
details:
The Constitution (81st Amendment) Act, 2000:
- Allowed the state to treat "backlog vacancies" for SC/STs as a separate class of vacancies, not subject to the 50% ceiling on annual reservations.

title: 82nd Amendment
gist: Permitted the state to lower qualifying marks or standards of evaluation for SC/STs in promotion.
details:
The Constitution (82nd Amendment) Act, 2000:
- Permitted the state to lower qualifying marks or standards of evaluation for SC/STs in matters of promotion.

title: 83rd Amendment
gist: Exempted Arunachal Pradesh from providing reservations for SCs in Panchayats.
details:
The Constitution (83rd Amendment) Act, 2000:
- Exempted Arunachal Pradesh from the requirement of providing reservations for SCs in Panchayats (as the state has no SC population).

title: 84th Amendment
gist: Extended the freeze on the total number of seats in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies until 2026.
details:
The Constitution (84th Amendment) Act, 2001:
- Extended the freeze on the total number of seats in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies until 2026, while allowing for delimitation based on the 1991 census.

title: 85th Amendment
gist: Provided for "consequential seniority" for SC/ST government servants in promotions.
details:
The Constitution (85th Amendment) Act, 2001:
- Provided for "consequential seniority" for SC/ST government servants in cases of promotion by virtue of rule of reservation.

title: 86th Amendment
gist: Made elementary education a Fundamental Right for children between 6-14 years.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
A landmark amendment that made elementary education a Fundamental Right, ensuring free and compulsory education for children.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (86th Amendment) Act, 2002
  • • Implementation: 12 December 2002
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Article 21A (New)Inserted to make free and compulsory education a Fundamental Right for children aged 6 to 14 years.
Article 45 (Amended)Changed the directive to focus on early childhood care and education for children below 6 years.
Article 51A(k) (New)Added an 11th Fundamental Duty for parents/guardians to provide educational opportunities to their children.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Laid the foundation for the Right to Education (RTE) Act, 2009.
  • Shifted education from a directive principle to a legally enforceable right.

title: 87th Amendment
gist: Provided that the delimitation of constituencies would be based on the 2001 Census.
details:
The Constitution (87th Amendment) Act, 2003:
- Provided that the delimitation of constituencies would be based on the 2001 Census instead of the 1991 Census.

title: 88th Amendment
gist: Formally introduced Service Tax as a constitutional provision.
details:
The Constitution (88th Amendment) Act, 2003:
- Formally introduced Service Tax as a constitutional provision (Article 268A).

title: 89th Amendment
gist: Bifurcated the combined National Commission for SCs and STs into two separate bodies.
details:
The Constitution (89th Amendment) Act, 2003:
- Bifurcated the combined National Commission for SCs and STs into two separate bodies: National Commission for SCs and National Commission for STs.

title: 90th Amendment
gist: Maintained the existing representation of STs and non-STs in the Bodoland Territorial Areas District.
details:
The Constitution (90th Amendment) Act, 2003:
- Maintained the existing representation of STs and non-STs in the Bodoland Territorial Areas District in Assam.

title: 91st Amendment
gist: Limited the size of the Council of Ministers to 15% and strengthened anti-defection laws.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Aimed at limiting the size of the Council of Ministers to prevent jumbo cabinets and further strengthening the Anti-Defection Law.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (91st Amendment) Act, 2003
  • • Implementation: 1 January 2004
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Article 75(1A) & 164(1A)Limited the total number of Ministers (including PM/CM) to 15% of the total strength of the Lok Sabha / State Assembly.
10th ScheduleRemoved the exception that allowed disqualification exemption in case of a "split" (one-third members) in a legislature party.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Rationalized government expenditure by preventing excessively large cabinets.
  • Made defection laws stricter by requiring at least a two-thirds majority (merger) for exemption.

title: 92nd Amendment
gist: Included Bodo, Dogri, Maithili, and Santhali in the Eighth Schedule.
details:
The Constitution (92nd Amendment) Act, 2004:
- Included Bodo, Dogri, Maithili, and Santhali in the Eighth Schedule.

title: 93rd Amendment
gist: Provided for reservations for SCs, STs, and OBCs in admission to private and public educational institutions.
details:
The Constitution (93rd Amendment) Act, 2006:
- Provided for reservations for SCs, STs, and OBCs in admission to private and public educational institutions (except minority institutions).

title: 94th Amendment
gist: Relieved Bihar from the obligation of having a Minister in charge of tribal welfare.
details:
The Constitution (94th Amendment) Act, 2006:
- Relieved Bihar from the obligation of having a Minister in charge of tribal welfare and extended the requirement to Jharkhand and Chhattisgarh.

title: 95th Amendment
gist: Extended SC/ST and Anglo-Indian reservations in legislatures for another 10 years.
details:
The Constitution (95th Amendment) Act, 2010:
- Extended SC/ST and Anglo-Indian reservations in legislatures for another 10 years (until 2020).

title: 96th Amendment
gist: Changed the spelling of "Oriya" to "Odia".
details:
The Constitution (96th Amendment) Act, 2011:
- Changed the spelling of "Oriya" to "Odia" and the language's name in the Eighth Schedule.

title: 97th Amendment
gist: Granted constitutional status and protection to Cooperative Societies.
details:
The Constitution (97th Amendment) Act, 2012:
- Granted constitutional status and protection to Cooperative Societies.

title: 98th Amendment
gist: Provided for special provisions for the Hyderabad-Karnataka region of the State of Karnataka.
details:
The Constitution (98th Amendment) Act, 2013:
- Provided for special provisions for the Hyderabad-Karnataka region of the State of Karnataka.

title: 99th Amendment
gist: Established the National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC).
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Aimed to replace the Collegium system of appointing judges with the National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC), though later struck down by the Supreme Court.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (99th Amendment) Act, 2014
  • • Implementation: 13 April 2015
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Art 124A, 124B, 124C (New)Inserted to establish the composition, functions, and parliamentary power regarding the NJAC.
Article 124 & 217 (Amended)Changed the appointment process of SC and HC judges to be based on NJAC recommendation instead of the Chief Justice's consultation.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Temporarily shifted power from the judiciary to the executive in judge appointments.
  • Struck down: In October 2015, the Supreme Court declared it unconstitutional for violating the "Basic Structure" (independence of judiciary).

title: 100th Amendment
gist: Ratified the Land Boundary Agreement (LBA) between India and Bangladesh.
details:
The Constitution (100th Amendment) Act, 2015:
- Ratified the Land Boundary Agreement (LBA) between India and Bangladesh, involving the exchange of enclaves.

title: 101st Amendment
gist: Goods and Services Tax (GST): Introduced a comprehensive indirect tax system across India.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Introduced a comprehensive indirect tax system across India, replacing multiple central and state taxes with a unified Goods and Services Tax (GST).
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (101st Amendment) Act, 2016
  • • Implementation: 1 July 2017
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Art 246A (New)Granted simultaneous power to Parliament and State Legislatures to make laws regarding GST.
Art 269A (New)Provided for the levy and collection of GST on inter-state trade by the Centre, apportioned with states.
Art 279A (New)Established the GST Council, a joint forum of the Centre and States.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Promoted cooperative federalism in taxation.
  • Subsumed taxes like Excise Duty, VAT, Service Tax, and Entry Tax into one GST.

title: 102nd Amendment
gist: Conferred constitutional status on the National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC).
details:
The Constitution (102nd Amendment) Act, 2018:
- Conferred constitutional status on the National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC).

title: 103rd Amendment
gist: EWS Reservation: Provided 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections in educational institutions and government jobs.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Introduced a 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) among the unreserved categories in education and public employment.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (103rd Amendment) Act, 2019
  • • Implementation: 14 January 2019
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Article 15(6) (New)Enabled the State to make special provisions for the advancement of any economically weaker sections of citizens (up to 10%) for admission to educational institutions.
Article 16(6) (New)Enabled the State to make provisions for reservation (up to 10%) in appointments or posts in favor of EWS.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Breached the historic 50% reservation ceiling set by the Supreme Court.
  • Introduced economic criteria as the sole basis for affirmative action for the first time.

title: 104th Amendment
gist: Extended reservation for SCs and STs in Lok Sabha and Assemblies for 10 years.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Extended the reservation of seats for SCs and STs in legislatures for another 10 years, while ending the nomination of Anglo-Indian members.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (104th Amendment) Act, 2019
  • • Implementation: 25 January 2020
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Article 334(a) (Amended)Extended the period of reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies to 80 years (up to 2030).
Article 334(b) (Lapsed)Did not extend the provision for the nomination of Anglo-Indian members, effectively abolishing it.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Ensured continued political representation for marginalized communities (SC/ST).
  • Ended the 70-year-old practice of reserving seats for the Anglo-Indian community.

title: 105th Amendment
gist: Restored the power of State Governments and UTs to identify their own lists of SEBCs.
details:
The Constitution (105th Amendment) Act, 2021:
- Clarified and restored the power of State Governments and UTs to identify and notify their own lists of Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBCs).

title: 106th Amendment
gist: Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam: Reserved one-third (33%) of seats for women in the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies.
details:
1. The 'Quick Glance':
Widely known as the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, this amendment reserves one-third of seats for women in the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies.
2. Key Legal Details:
  • • Act Name: The Constitution (106th Amendment) Act, 2023
  • • Implementation: 28 September 2023 (Notified)
3. Article-wise Changes:
ProvisionKey Changes
Art 239AA (Amended)Extended 33% women's reservation to the Legislative Assembly of Delhi.
Art 330A (New)Reserved 33% seats in the Lok Sabha for women, including horizontal reservation within SC/ST quotas.
Art 332A (New)Reserved 33% seats in all State Legislative Assemblies for women.
Art 334A (New)Implementation trigger: Valid for 15 years after the first post-act Census and Delimitation.
4. Functional Impact:
  • Guarantees 33% representation for women in direct elections to the Lower House and State Assemblies.
  • Does not apply to Rajya Sabha or State Legislative Councils.
